## List of Illustrations

## Colour Plates

I. Two bonnets from Slovak folk costume. 19th century. Oxford, Private Collection.

II. The nest of the bower bird. From Karl von Frisch, Animal Architecture, Harcourt Brace Jovanovich, New York and London, 1974, pl. 99. Photo © Dr Max Renner, Munich.

III. Bark cloth from Tongatabu. From Owen Jones, The Grammar of Ornament, London, 1856, pl. 1.

IV. Peruvian textile, 6th-11th century. British Museum, Museum of Mankind, by courtesy of the Trustees.

V. Star Ushak carpet (detail). Persian, about 1600. New York, Metropolitan Museum of Art, gift of Joseph V. McMullan, 1958.

VI. Mirador of Lindaraja, Alhambra, Granada. 14th century. Photo Mas, Barcelona.

VII. The Ixworth Cross, found at Stanton, near Ixworth, Suffolk. About A.D. 6oo. Oxford, Ashmolean Museum.

VIII. Kaleidoscope patterns from a view of the Senate House of the University of London and a telephone box. Photographed for the author by Mr S. Parker-Ross.

IX. From the English edition of M. E. Chevreul, The Laws of Contrast of Colour, London, 1861, pl. xv. Originally published Paris, 1839, as De la loi du contraste simultané des couleurs.

X. Detail of Imperial Dragon robe (Chi-fu) with 12 symbols, worn exclusively by the Chinese Emperor, late 18th to 19th century. London, Victoria and Albert Museum.

XI. Jean le Haze: 'Mille-fleurs' tapestry made for Philip the Good, Duke of Burgundy. Brussels, 1466. Bern, Historisches Museum.

## Black and White Plates

1. Raphael: Madonna della Sedia, about I516, in a frame of about I7oo. Florence, Palazzo Pitti. Photo Ediz. Jacquier.

2. The Michaelerplatz, Vienna, with the façade of Goldman and Salatsch by Adolf Loos (begun 1909) on the left, and that of St. Michael's Church (18th century) on the right. Photo courtesy of W. Hlawatsch, Vienna.

3. Tiled floor from Clarendon Palace. About 1250. London, British Museum, by courtesy of the Trustees.

4. Woman's hat from Yurok, Trinidad Bay, Panama. Vienna, Museum für Völkerkunde.

5. Gateway to the South Court of the Temple at Srirangam, near Trichinopoly, India. I7th century. Photo Archaeological Survey of India, c. 187o, by courtesy of the India Office Library.

6. The interior of the Cartuja in Granada. 1727-64. Photo Courtauld Institute.

7. Interior of the Badia of Fiesole, 14607. Photo Alinari, Florence.

8. One of a sct of four gilded bronze wall lights. French, about 175o. New York, Metropolitan Museum of Art, gift of Mr and Mrs Charles Wrightsman.

9. F. A. Krubsacius: Mock cartouche. From Gedanken von dem Ursprung, Wachstum und Verfall der Verzierungen in den schönen Künsten (Leipzig, 1759).

I0. Soup tureen. French, middle of the eighteenth century. Paris, Musée des Arts Décoratifs.

I1. 'Ornaments of Savage Tribes'. From Owen Jones, The Grammar of Ornament, London, 1856, pl. 3.

12. H. W. Phillips: Owen Jones. 1857. London, Royal Institute of British Architects.

I3. Bear claw necklace of the Pawnee Indians. Vienna, Museum für Völkerkunde.

14. Aborigines from Mount Hagen, New Guinea, in festive attire. From Andrew and Marilyn Strathern, Self-Decoration in Mount Hagen, London, 1972. Photograph by the authors.

15. Grinling Gibbons: Detail of reredos, Trinity College, Oxford. About 170o. Photo by Thomas-Photos, Oxford. Reproduced by permission of Trinity College.

16. Grinling Gibbons: Lace cravat carved in limewood. London, Victoria and Albert Museum.

17. Italian needlepoint (point de neige). Second half of the I7th century. London, Victoria and Albert Museum.

18a. Adam Krafft: Sakramentshäuschen, St. Lorenzkirche, Nuremberg, 1493-6. Photo Courtauld Institute.

18b. Adam Krafft: Detail of the Sakramentshäuschen (Plate 18a). Photo Bildarchiv Foto Marburg.

19. Stalls, Lancaster Priory. About I34O. Photo courtesy of Whitworth Art Gallery, University of Manchester.

20. Balustrade of organ loft, Ottobeuren. About 1766. From Max Seidel, Unbekanntes Barock, Ottobeuren, Stuttgart, 1976. Reproduced by permission of Max Seidel.

21. Silver filigree slipper decoration from Tunisia. Stuttgart, Linden Museum.

22. Ivory armlet of the Oba of Benin. Nigeria, 16th century. London, British Museum, Museum of Mankind, by courtesy of the Trustees.

23, 24.From W. A. Bentley and W. J. Humphreys, Snow Crystals, 1931; reprinted by Dover Publications, New York, 1962, pp. I46-7. Reproduced by permission of McGraw-Hill Book Company.

2. Red-figured Attic cup, with figures of Dionysos and Ariadnc escorted by Eros. 400-390 B.C. Found at Nola, Italy. London, British Museum, by courtesy of the Trustees.

26. Chinese couch, yellow rosewood and cane. Ming dynasty, I7th century. Portland Art Museum, Oregon, gift of Margery Hoffman Smith.

27. Detail of a golden cup from Koul Oba tomb, Crimea. Greek workmanship, 4th century B.C. Leningrad, Hermitage.

28. Stalactite dome, Hall of the Two Sisters, Alhambra, Granada, I4th century. Photo Mas, Barcclona.

29. Choir vault, Tewkesbury Abbey. About 1350. Photo Crown Copyright, National Monuments Record.

30. Dctail of decoration bordering the entrance to the Salon de Comarcs, the Alhambra, Granada, I 4th century. From E. G. Gómez andJ. B. Pareja, La Alhambra: la Casa Real, Granada, 1969.

31. The Mausoleum of the Saadicns, Marrakesh. 16th century. From H. Glück and E. Diez, Die Kunst des Islam, Berlin, 1925, p. 201.

32, 33. Chi-Rho page from the Book of Kells, and detail. Early 9th century. Dublin, Trinity College, reproduced by permission of the Board.

34. The choir of Monreale Cathedral, Sicily. End of the 12th century. Photo Courtauld Institute, © Tim Benton.

35. Marble slabs on a detail of revetment and frieze. Jerusalem, Dome of the Rock, outer ambulatory. Completed A.D. 692. Photo © B. Brenk.

36. Mirza Akbar: Drawings from a pattern book. Persian, 19th century. No. 44, star tracery lines for wooden windows; no. 32, sketches for groined vaultings showing auxiliary lines. London, Victoria and Albert Museum.

37, 38. J. F. Lewis: The Door of the Hall of Ambassadors, and the Torre de las Infantas (Alhambra). 1833. London, Victoria and Albert Museum.

39a. Botticelli: Detail from the Adoration of theKings. About .London, National Gally.

39b. Canaletto: Detail from The Basin of S. Marco on Ascension Day. About 1740. London, National Gallery.

40. Celtic bronze mirror from Desborough. Ist century A.D. London, British Museum, by courtesy of the Trustees.

1Blue and white pilgrim bottle. Chinese, Ming dynasty, early 15th century. London, Percival David Foundation.

42. Sutra storage box. Japanese, Edo period, 17th century. Cornell University, Ithaca, N.Y., Herbert F. Johnson Museum of Art, George and Mary Rockwell Collection.

Silv tur yBultonan Fotergil7. Birmingham, Assay Office.

4.Litte Moreton Hall, Cheshire. 16th century. Photo by courtesy of the Whitworth Art Gallery, University of Manchester.

case). Greek workmanship, late sth-early 4th century B.c. Rostov-on-Don, Museum.

51. Romanesque capital from the crypt of St. Servatius, Quedlinburg, consecrated 1129. From O. Beyer, Romanik, Berlin, 1923, pl. 34.

52. Capital from the triforium, Rheims Cathedral. About 1250-60. Photo © James Austin.

53. Capital from the Chapter House, Southwell Minster. About 1330. Photo Courtauld Institute, © F. H. Crossley and Canon M. H. Ridgway.

54. Tomb relief slabs. Chinese, later Han dynasty, A.D. I5o. Toronto, Royal Ontario Museum.

55. Chinese vase. Sung dynasty, 12th-13th century. Toronto, Royal Ontario Museum.

56. Detail of a Buddha. Indian, Gupta period, sth century A.D. Calcutta, Indian Museum. From Indische Kunst, catalogue of an exhibition held at the Württembergischer Kunstverein, Stuttgart, and the Museum fur Kunst und Gewerbe, Hamburg, 1966.

57. Relief from the Hoysaleswara Temple, Halebid, India, 1141-82. Photo by Julieta Preston, courtesy of the Indian Tourist Office.

58. Detail of the Ara Pacis Augustac. Rome, 13-9 B.C. Photo Alinari, Florence.

59. Rose pillar from the tomb of the Haterii. Ist century A.D. Rome, Vatican Museums. Photo Anderson, Alinari, Florence.

60. Detail from a panel of Marcus Aurelius. About A.D. 175. Rome, Palazzo dei Conservatori. Photo Deutsches Archaeologisches Institut, Rome.

61. Gregor Erhart: High altar of the Klosterkirche, Blaubeuren, Germany. 1493-4. Photo Bildarchiv Foto Marburg.

62. German wardrobe. About 1500. Munich, Bayerisches Nationalmuseum.

63. Italian chest. About 1540. Formerly Berlin, Kaiser-Friedrich Museum.

64. Lucas Cranach: St. Valentine and Donor. About 1503. Vienna, Akademie der Bildenden Künste.

65. Vermeer: Detail from A Young Woman standing at a Virginal. About 1670. London, National Gallery.

66. Giotto: Decorative panel from the Arena Chapel, Padua. About 1306. Photo Kunsthistorisches Institut, Florence.

67. Capital from Saint-Julien, Brioude, Haute-Loire. 12th century. Photo © Dr Z. Swiechowski.

68. Revetment from Notre-Dame, Louviers. Late I5th century. Photo Courtauld Institute.

69. Ionic capital from the Erechtheion. About 415 B.C. London, British Museum, by courtesy of the Trustees.

70. Capitals from the Temple of Bacchus, Baalbek, Lebanon, A.D. 138-61. Photo Courtauld Institute.

71. Capital from S. Apollinare in Classe, Ravenna. A.D. 534-9. Photo Courtauld Institute.

72. Chapel of Henry VII, Westminster Abbey. 1503-19. Photo Crown Copyright, National Monuments Record.

73. Lucas von Hildebrandt: The Hall of Mirrors, Lower Belvedere, Vienna. About 1725. Photo © Bundesdenkmalamt, Vienna.

7 Porta dei Bombardieri, Verona. 1687. Photos by Pino Guidolotti.

75. T'ao-t'ieh on bronze vessel (fang γi). Chinese Shang dynasty, 12th-11th century B.C. London, British Museum, by courtesy of the Trustees.

76 Black velvet cope from the treasures of Charles the Bold, Duke of Burgundy. 15th century. Fribourg, Switzerland, Musée d'art et d'histoire; on display in the château at Gruyères.

77. Ford Madox Brown: Work. 1852-65. Manchester, City Art Gallery.

78. Bowl with Cufic decoration. Nishapur, Iran, 1oth century. New York, Metropolitan Museum of Art, Rogers Fund.

79. Bowl from Isnik, Turkey, about A.D. 1500. London, British Museum, by courtesy of the Trustees.

81. Gianlorenzo Bernini: Cathedra, St. Peter's Basilica, Rome. 165766.Photo© Phaidon Press.

82. Cross page from the Lindisfarne Gospel. About 700. London, British Library, Cotton MS. Nero D iv, fol. 26v. Reproduced by permission of the British Library Board.

83. Page from the Luttrell Psalter. About 1340. London, British Library, Add. MS. 42 130, foi. 196v. Reproduced by permission of the British Library Board.

84. Page from a Flemish calendar. Early 16th century. London, British Library, Add. MS. 24098, fol. 27b. Reproduced by permission of the British Library Board.

85. Details of pilasters from the Grotte Vaticane, Rome. About A.D. 200. Photo Alinari, Florence.

86. Gold openwork dagger handle. Chinese, late Chou dynasty, 4th century B.C. London, British Museum, by courtesy of the Trustees.

87. Bronze vessel (kuei). Chinese, Shang dynasty, 12th-IIth century B.C. London, British Museum, by courtesy of the Trustees.

88. Details from the Book of Kells. Early 9th century. Fol. 76v.; fol. 272r.; fol. 230v.; detail of Chi-Rho page, fol. 34r. Dublin, Trinity College; reproduced by permission of the Board.

## Figures in the Text

## Introduction

1. Fairy ring. Drawing by M. Wenzel.

2. Partridge. Drawing by M. Wenzel.

3. Duck. Drawing by M. Wenzel.

4. Cicatrization. Drawing by M. Wenzel. A man from Lakata, after G. Buschan, Die Sitten der Völker, p. 103.

5. Sea urchin. Photo A. C. Cooper.

6. Cyclopean walls. From W. Lübke, Geschichte der Architektur, Cologne, 1858. p. 70.

7. Crazy pavement. Drawing by M. Wenzel.

8. Flagstones. Drawing by M. Wenzel.

9. Parquet flooring. Drawing by M. Wenzel.

10. Fan-pattern by Congo. From Desmond Morris, The Biology of Art, London, 1962, fig. 28.

11. Reversed fan-pattern by Congo, same source, fig. 29.

12. Obliterated pattern by Congo, same source, fig. 44.

13. Two drawing procedures. From Walter Crane, Line and Form, London, 1902, p. 11.

14. Raphael: Studies of the Virgin and Child. About 1505. London, British Museum, by courtesy of the Trustees.

I5. Raphael: Part of a sheet of studies for the Alba Madonna. About 15o8. Lille, Musée Wicar.

## I Issues of Taste

16. Wedding cake. Drawing by M. Wenzel.

17. Japanese bowl. Stoneware. Shino, early Edo period, 17th or early 18th century. Ithaca, N.Y., H. F. Johnson Museum of Art, George and Mary Rockwell Collection.

18. The Temple of Jupiter on the Capitol, from a denarius of Petillius Capitolinus, about 37 B.C. Drawing by M. Wenzel, after G. Fuchs, Architekturdarstellungen auf Römischen Münzen der Republik und der frühen Kaiserzeit, Berlin, 1969.

19. Giuliano da Sangallo: Drawing after Roman grotesques in the Domus Aurea, about I 5o5, from Taccuino Senese, fol. 4o. Siena, Biblioteca Comunale. Photo Comm. Brandi, Siena. Reproduced by permission of N. Dacos.

20. Marie Antoinette's library at Versailles. About 1783. Photo Musées Nationaux, Paris.

21. St. Paul's Cathedral, London. From C. Wren, Parentalia, 1750.

22. Music Room, Sanssouci, Potsdam, begun 1746. Photo Archiv für Kunst und Geschichte, Berlin.

23. Designs by F. de Cuvilliès, engraved by Lespilliez. About I740. London, Victoria and Albert Museum.

24. Façade of Saint-Sulpice by Servandoni and others begun 1733. From F. Blondel, Architecture Françoise, Book 11I, no. 5, pl. 2, 1752. Photo Courtauld Institute.

25. Wall decoration from Herculaneum. From Le Pitture Antiche d'Erculaneo, 1757, pl. 217.

26. Piranesi: Design for a chimney piece. From Divers Manners of Ornamenting Chimneys, 1769, pl. 28. Photo Victoria and Albert Museum.

27. Milan Cathedral, founded 1386, from R. Töpffer, Voyages en Zig-Zag, 4th ed., 1855.

28.J. E. Nilson: 'Neues Caffehaus'. Engraving, 1756. From Jessen, vol. 11, p. 149.

29. J. E. Nilson: 'Tearing up the Rocaille'. Engraving, about 1770. Courtesy of Professor Hermann Bauer.

30. The Apothecary's Grotto. Drawing by M. Wenzel. The artist has combined motifs from German Rococo monuments: the grotto from

Schloss Weissenstein in Pommersfelden; the gnones from Schloss Öttingen; the painting from Pesne murals in Charlottenburg.

31. Writing table of inlaid satin wood. About 1780. London, Victoria and Albert Museum.

32. Chair fron C. Percier and P. F. L. Fontaine, Recueil de décorations intérieures, Paris, 1812, pl. 29.

## II Ornament as Art

33. A. W. Pugin: 'Crace Diaper' design for a wallpaper for the House of Commons, produced by Samuel Scott for J. G. Crace, about 1848. London, Victoria and Albert Museum.

34. Flowered carpet by Messrs. T. Smith and Co., from the Catalogue of the Great Exhibition, 1851.

35. 'Patterns of Brumagem Gothic', from A. W. Pugin, The True Principles of Pointed or Christian Architecture, London, 1841.

36. 'Ornamental abominations', from R. Wornum, The Analysis of Ornament, 1856.

37. Glass flower vase, from the printed catalogue of the 'Department of Practical Art', Articles of Ornamental Art . . . for the use of Students and Manufacturers and the consultation of the Public, 1852. Photo Victoria and Albert Museum.

38. Egg-and-dart moulding. From R. Wornum, The Analysis of Ornament, 1856.

39. Cast-iron hall stove, by Messrs. Baily and Sons ("novel in design and bold in its detail'), from the Catalogue of the Great Exhibition, 1851.

40.J. Ruskin, Seven Lamps, pl. xII: Lombard ornament.

41.J. Ruskin, Seven Lamps, pl. 1: part of the Cathedral of Saint-Lô, Normandy.

42. J. Ruskin, Seven Lamps, pl. xiv: sculptures from the bas-reliefs on the North door of Rouen Cathedral.

. Biscuit barrel. English, about 1965. Photo A. C. Cooper.

44. J. Ruskin, 'Temperance and intemperance in curvature', The Stones of Venice, vol. 11, pl. I, 1853.

45. Floral ornament from Ajanta, Maharastra cave I, 6th century A.D. From G. Yazdani et al., Ajanta, Oxford, 1930-55, vol. 11. Reproduced by permission of the Director of Archaeology and Museums, Andhra Pradesh, and Oxford University Press.

46. G. Semper: Design for a punchbowl. From Der Stil, 1860.

47. Elements of ornament in costume. Drawing by M. Wenzel.

Festive cloth used as a handkerchief and to cover gifts at wedings. Muslin embroidered with silks. Chamba, Punjab Hills, Himachal Pradesh, 18th century. London, Victoria and Albert Museum.

Shirt of theBlackfoot Indians. Vienna Musum für Völkerkunde.

5o. Modern lcather garment. Drawing by M. Wenzel.

1.Chair  thT ors  n Camden Arts Centre, London. Photo Frank Schofield.

52. Two Egyptian chairs. From G. Semper, Der Stil, 1860.

53. Tattooed head from New Zealand. From Owen Jones, The Grammar of Ornament, 1856, p. I4.

54-6. From Owen Jones, The Grammar of Ornament, 1856, pp. 15, 67, 68.

57. 'Leaves from nature'. From Owen Jones, The Grammar of Ornament, pl. XCI.

59,60. Japanese bottle and bowl. From L. Gonse, L'Art Japonais, Paris, 1885.

63. 'The foundation of the Fenollosa System of Art Education'. From W. Beck, Self-Development in Drawing, New York and London, 1928, fig. 92.

64, 65. Straw stars. From Gretl Zimmermann, Neue Strohsterne und Engel. English edition, Straw Stars, trans. by R. Albrecht, Freiburg im Breisgau, 1968, fig. 42 and fig. 29.

66. Locksmith's sign, wrought-iron, gilded. German, about 175o. London, Victoria and Albert Museum.

67. The five Platonic bodies. From Manfred Eigen and Ruthild Winkler, Das Spiel, Munich, 1975, p. 130. Reproduced by permission of R.Piper & Co. Verlag, Munich.

68. Translation, rotation, reflection.

69. Phoenician ivory, about 9th century B.c. London, British Museum, by courtesy of the Trustees.

70. Triangles and hexagon grids, from Lewis F. Day, The Anatony of Pattern, London 1890, pls. 6, 8.

71. The seventeen symmetries, from Andreas Speiser, Die Tleorie der endlichen Gruppen, 3rd ed., Zurich, 1937. Reprinted by Dover Publications, New York, 1955, figs. 1733.

72. Seven border types, from Andreas Speiser, same source, figs. 511.

73-8. Douat's method of permutation, after S. Truchet, Méthode pour faire une infinité de desseins différents . . . ou observations du Père Dominic Douat Religieux Carme ..., Paris, 1722.

79. Diagram from Charles Blanc, Art in Ornament and Dress, London, 1877, p. 45.

8. From C. P. Hornung, Handbook of Designs and Devices, New York, 1932. Reprinted by Dover Publications, New York, 1946.

81. Tabulations from Wolfgang von Wersin, Das elementare Ornament und seine Gesetzlichkeit, 3rd ed., Ravensburg, 1953. © 1953 Otto Maier Verlag, Ravensburg. Drawing by Herthe von Wersin.

82. The north-west corner of the Parthenon. Drawing by M. Wenzel.

83. The Doric order, Roman version. From W. Lübke, Geschiclte der Architektur, Cologne, 1858, fig. 92.

84. Egyptian ceiling pattern from Thebes. 19th

dynasty, 1348-1315 B.C. From N. M. Davies, Ancient Egyptian Paintings, Chicago, 1936, pl. 84.

85. Reconstruction of design procedure. From A. H. Christie, Pattern Design, 1929. Reprinted by Dover Publications, New York, 1969, fig. I11.

86. Diagram of progressive filling in.

87. Golden cup from Koul Oba tomb, Crimea, 4th century B.c. From Alfred Maskell, Russian Art, London, 1884, pl. 5.

88. Tile facings in the Hall of the Ambassadors, Granada. From A. Speltz, The Styles of Ornament, 1910, pl. 119, nos. I and 2.

89. Lace pattern, 'carreau en ronde'. From J. Foillet, Pourtraicts de point coupé, 1598.

90. From R. W. Billings, The Power of Form Applied to Tracery, London, 1851.

91. From J. Albers and F. Bucher, Trotz der Geraden, Bern, 1961. Reproduced by permission of Benteli Verlag, Bern.

92. Construction of a knotwork panel. From G. Bain, Celtic Art: The Method of Construction, Glasgow, 1944, p. 1.

93. Engraving after Leonardo da Vinci, about 1495. From A. M. Hind, Early Italian Engravings, London, 1948, vol. vI, pl. 628, knot no. 5.

94. Leonardo da Vinci: Drawing from the Codice Atlantico. From he facsimile edition, Florence, 1974, vol. vI, pl. 520.

95. Venetian-Saracenic bowl. Brass inlaid with silver. Early 16th century. London, Victoria and Albert Museum.

96. Woodcut by Albrecht Dürer, 1507. From Ernst Lehner, Alphabets and Ornaments, 1952; reprinted by Dover Publications, New York, 1968, p. 151.

97. Interlace patterns. From J. Bourgoin, Arabic Geometrical Pattern and Design, Dover Publications, New York, 1973, pp. 30, 42 (originally published 1879).

98. 'The drop', from Lewis F. Day, The Anatomy of Pattern, 1890, pl. 25.

99. M. C. Escher: Counterchange of fish and frog. About 1942. From Caroline H. MacGillavry, Symmetry Aspects of M. C. Escher's Periodic Drawings, Utrecht, 1965, pl. 1b.

100. Matwork by Brazilian Indians. From Gene Weltfish, The Origins of Art, Indianapolis, 1953, pl. xx.

101. Amazon Indian club. From Hjalmar Stolpe, Amazon Indian Designs, 1927. Reprint ed. T. Menten, Dover Publications, New York, 1974, p. 8.

102. Ancient Mexican stamp. From Jorge Enciso, Design Motifs of Ancient Mexico, English language edition, 1947; Dover Publications, New York, 1953.

103. Islamic counterchange. From C. B. Griesbach, Historic Ornament: A Pictorial Archive (n.d.), Dover Publications, New York, 1975, fig. 4.

104. Wall decoration in the Hall of the Ambassadors, Alcazar, Seville, I4th century. Photo Mas, Barcelona.

105. Michio Kubo: Counterchange. From Hide and Seek, Osaka, 1968.

106. Cut-out patterns. From F. Cizek, Papier-Schneide und Klebearbeiten, Vienna, 1916.

107. Glass panels. From Félibien, Traité de l'Architecture, 1676, book 1, p. 193.

232. Pheasant border from a Persian silk carpet, Kashan, mid-16th century. New York, Metropolitan Museum of Art, bequest of Benjamin Altman, 1913.

233a. Capital from St. John in Constantinople. Sth century A.D. From Riegl, Stilfragen, fig. 142.

233b. Capital from the Hagia Sophia, Constantinople. 6th century A.D. From Riegl, Stilfragen, fig. 145.

234. Analysis of the arabesque, from Riegl, Stilfragen, fig. 139.

235. Acanthus scroll on a stone relief from the church of SS. Sergius and Bacchus, Constantinople, A.D. 52736. From A. Speltz, The Styles of Ornament, pl. 66.

236. Carved Head from Tanimbar, Moluccan Islands. Leiden, Ethnological Museum.

237. Cloud band. Drawing by M. Wenzel.

238. Paisley pattern. From R. Glazier, Historic Textile Fabrics, 1923, pl. 40.

239. Drawing by Saul Steinberg. From The New World (Harper & Row). © 1964 Saul Steinberg. Originally in The New Yorker. Reproduced by courtesy of the artist.

## VIII The Psychology of Styles

240. Openwork, about 3rd century A.D. From Riegl, Kunstindustrie, pl. xV.

241. Chipcarving, about Sth century A.D. From Riegl, Kunstindustrie, pl. xx.

242. Gothic ornament. From A. Speltz, The Styles of Ornament, pl. 180.

243. Hans Schönsperger: From the first German law book. Augsburg, I496. From Ernst Lehner, Alphabets and Ornaments, 1952; reprinted by Dover Publications, New York, 1968, p. 25.

244. Erhard Ratdolt: title page. Venice, 1476. From Ernst Lehner, same source as above, p. 15.

245, 246. Gothic and Renaissance shoes. From Viollet-le-Duc, Dictionnaire du Mobilier Français, Paris, 185875, vol. IV, pp. 337 and 340.

247. Martin Schongauer (d. 1491): Gothic ornamental leaf. London, British Museum, by courtesy of the Trustees.

248. Wolf Huber: Pen drawing. About 1520. Göttingen University.

249. Tapestry of St. Michael, Halberstadt Cathedral. German, second half of the 12th century.

250, 251. J. Ruskin: Seven Lamps of Architecture, The Lamp of Power, pp. 889.

252. Example of the Perpendicular style, from T. Rickman, An Attempt to Discriminate The Styles of Arclitecture in England, London, 4th ed., 1836, pl. Ix.

253. The Divinity Schools, Oxford. About 1440. Photo by Thomas-Photos, Oxford.

25. Pierre Lepautre: Engraving of a chimney piece for Marly. From the Livre des Cheminées Executées à Marly, 1699. New York, Metropolitan Museum of Art, Harris Brisbane Dick Fund.

255. Chimney piece from Pierre-Jean Mariette's edition of A. C. D'Aviler, Cours d' Architecture qui comprend les Ordres de Vignole avec des commentaires, Paris, 1760 (frst published 1735): 'Cheminée propre pour un cabinet', pl. 57, p. 191.

256. The façade of the Petit Trianon, Versailles. 17628. Photo Giraudon, Paris.

257. Contrasted royal chapels: The Chapel Royal Brighton, and St. George's Chapel, Windsor, from A. W. Pugin, Contrasts, or a Parallel between the Noble Edifices of the fourteenth and fifteenth centuries and similar buildings of the present day, London, 1836.

## IX Designs as Signs

258. Music stand. Drawing by M. Wenzel.

Trophy.From A.Speltz, The Styles Omet,pl. 362.

260. The School of Tropical Medicine, London. 19268. Drawing by M. Wenzel.

261. B. F. Latrobe: Corn and tobacco capitals

designed for the Capitol in Washington (1809 and 1817). From G. Brown, History of the U.S. Capitol, 2 vols., Washington, 19003.

262. Priest of the Solar Ophite religion, from north west of Louisiana, after a drawing made in 1741. From J. B. Deane, The Worship of the Serpent traced througlout the world, 2nd ed., London, 1833.

263. Designs on prehistoric pottery. From J. B. Waring, Ceramic Art in Remote Ages, London, 1874, pl. 27.

264. Derivatives of an alligator after W. H. Holmes. From A.C. Haddon, Evolution in Art: as Illustrated by the Life-History of Design, London, 1895, fig. 96.

265. Transformations of a motif in North American tribal art. From F. Boas, Primitive Art, Oslo, 1927, fig. I17. Reprinted by Dover Publications, New York, 1955.

266. Javanese Batik design, 'parang rusak'. London, Victoria and Albert Museum.

267. Developments in uniforms. From O. Koenig, Kultur und Verhaltensforsclung, Munich, 1970. Reproduced by permission of Deutscher Taschenbuch Verlag, Munich.

268. Coffee bar. Drawing by M. Wenzel.

269. Pub. Drawing by M. Wenzel.

270. Christmas wrapping paper. Drawing by M. Wenzel.

271. Heraldic terminology. From A. C. Fox-Davies, A Complete Guide to Heraldry, London, 1909, figs. 65-73.

272. The Valvasone coat of arms. Early 17th century. London, Victoria and Albert Museum.

273. Royal coat of arms. From R. Pricke, The Ornaments of Architecture, 1674.

274. Wendel Dietterlin: Coat of arms with human supporters. From The Fantastic Engravings of Wendel Dietterlin, 203 Plates and Text of his Architecture (1958). Reprinted by Dover Publications, New York, 1968.

275. Egyptian chair. Boxwood and acacia, 18th dynasty, 1551-1306 B.c. New York, Metropolitan Museum of Art, Rogers Fund, 1936.

276. Floral unguent vase from the tomb of Tutankhamun (d. c. 1352 B.C.).

277. Characters from a poem by Tao-chi (1640-c. 1717). Ch'ing dynasty. London, Collection of Madame Ling Su-hua.

278. Chinese lattice screen, Chengtu Szechwan, A.D. 1900. From Daniel Sheets Dye, Chinese Lattice Designs, Dover Publications, New York, 1974, p. 355.

279. Cufic script from the Koran. Mesopotamia, 17th century. From Ernst Lehner, Alphabets and Ornanents, 1952, reprinted by Dover Publications, New York, 1968. p. 214.

280. Drawing by Saul Steinberg. From The Passport (Harper & Bros.). © 1953 Saul Steinberg. Originally in The New Yorker. Reproduced by courtesy of the artist.

281. Thermae. Inscription from Pompeii. Naples, Museo Nazionale.

282. Jan van den Velde: Calligraphic page. Rotterdam, 1605. From Ernst Lehner, same source as fig. 279, p. 198.

283a. Edward Cocker: Magnum in Parvo. 1672. London, Victoria and Albert Museum.

283b. Edward Cocker: Scroll. London, 1657. From E Lehner, sm urc  ...

284. Monogram. From H. & B. Cirker, Monograms and Alphabetic Devices, Dover Publications, New York, 1970.

285. Signature of Queen Elizabeth I. London, Victoria and Albert Museum.

286. Antonello da Messina: 'Cartello' from Salvator Mundi, about 1465. London, National Gallery.

287. Dürer: Detail from the Triumphal Arch of Maximilian. Woodcut, 1515.

288. J. U. Krauss: The Ascension of Enoch and the Loading of the Ark. About 170o. From J. U. Krauss, Baroque Cartouches, Dover Publications, New York, 1969, p. 4.

289. J. U. Krauss: The Flood. About 170o. Same source as fig. 288, p. S.

290. Balustrade from the Cabinet des Médailles, Wallace Collection, London. 173341. Iron and bronze, chased and gilt. London, Wallace Collection, by courtesy of the Trustees.

291. Egyptian solar disk. From the back of the cedarwood chair of Tutankhamun. Cairo, Museum. Photo Hirmer Verlag, Munich.

292. Griffin-demons and sacred tree. Relief from Nimrud, 9th century B.c. London, British Museum, by courtesy of the Trustees. Photo Mansell Collection.

293. Spanish textile. 12th century. London, Victoria and Albert Museum.

294. Transylvanian cross-stitch design. From Folk Cross Stitch Design, collected by E. Sigerius, introduction by H. E. Kiewe, Oxford, 1964. Reproduced by permission of H. E. Kiewe.

295. Sumerian and Romanesque motifs compared. From J. Baltruaitis, Art Sumérien, Art Roman, Paris, 1934, p. 21, fgs. 9a and b; and p. 23, gs. I Ia and b.

296. Indian shri-yantra mandala. From H. Zimmer, Kunstform und Yoga im Indischen Kultbild, Berlin, 1920, pl. 36.

297. The cross-shaped motif before 200o B.c. From Flinders Petrie, Decorative Patterns of the Ancient World, London, 1930, pl. Lv11. Reproduced by permission of Ann Flinders Petrie.

298. Golden and bejewelled cover of a Gospel book, presented by Pope Gregory I to the Lombard Queen Theodolinda. About 6oo. Italy, Monza Cathedral. From H. Glück, Die Christliche Kunst des Ostens, Berlin, 1923. Reproduced by permission of Bruno Cassirer.

299. Wrought-iron grave cross. German, 16th century. London, Victoria and Albert Museum.

300. Helmet of the Household Cavalry. From a London Transport poster.

## X The Edge of Chaos

301, 302. Albrecht Dürer: Prayer to St. Apollonia, and Prayer to the Virgin. From the prayer-book of the Emperor Maximilian. 1515. Munich, Bayerische Staatsbibliothek (margins after G. Hirth, Munich, 1885).

303. Philippe de Mazerolles: Pen flourishes from a document issued by Charles the Bold, Duke of Burgundy. About 470. Vienna, Nationalbibliothek, Cod. Vinc. Ser. n. 2616, fol. 6v7r.

304. Capitals from the crypt of Canterbury Cathedral. Early 12th century. Photo Courtauld Institute, © Professor G. Zarnecki.

305. Mosaic floor from the Bath of Neptune, Ostia. Mid ist century A.D.

306. Guardian bull from the doorway in the South courtyard in the North-West Palace at Nimrud. About 880 B.c. London, British Museum, by courtesy of the Trustees.

307. Breastplate. Greek, late Sth century B.c. Leningrad, Hermitage.

308. Cretan pot. About 15oo B.c. Drawing by M. Wenzel after Spyros Marinatos and Max Hirmer, Crete and Mycenae, London, 1960, pl. 87.

309. Jug found in the City of London. 14th century. London, British Museum, by courtesy of the Trustees.

3 10. Painted pot from New Guinea. Stuttgart, Linden Museum.

3 11. Pot from Nubia. Aswan, Museum. From Silvio Curto, Nubia, Novara, 1965, colour plate vIII.

312. Bronze ritual vessel, fang yi. Chinese, Western Chou period, ist millennium B.C., excavated at Furfeng, Shensi. Institute of Archaeology, Chinese Academy of Sciences. Photo Robert Harding Associates, London.

31 3. Censer from Quen Santo, Guatemala. Probably late Old Maya, Ist millennium A.D. Berlin, Museum fur Völkerkunde. From E. von Sydow, Die Kunst der Naturvölker und der Vorzeit, Berlin, 1932, p. 407.

3 14. Dragon termination from the roof of a model Ming house. 14th or 15th century. London, British Museum, by courtesy of the Trustees.

315. Dragon's head termination from a pyramid at Teotihuacán. Early Ist millennium A.D. Drawing by M. Wenzel.

316. Dragon's head termination from the roof of a house in Serfaus, Tyrol. Drawing by M. Wenzel.

3 17. Hilt of a beaten silver sword from Ceylon. 18th century. Drawing by M. Wenzel after Karl Saelzle, Tier und Mensch, Munich, 1965.

318. Bronze from Luristan. Early Ist millennium B.C. Paris, Louvre. Photo Musées Nationaux, Paris.

319. Fragment of bronze and iron scabbard. Late 4th century B.C. Found at Filottrano, Italy. Ancona, Museo Nazionale delle Marche. Photo E. Neuffer, reproduced by courtesy of Professor J. V. S. Megaw.

320. Detail of a bronze neck-ring from Barbuise (Aube). Late 4th or early 3rd century B.C. Troyes, Musée. Photo J. Bienaimé, Troyes; reproduced by courtesy of Professor J. V. S. Megaw.

321. Detail of a pair of hollow gold bracelets from Waldalgesheim, Germany. Late 4th century B.C. Bonn, Landesmuseum. Photo © Professor J. V. S. Megaw.

322. Variants of the 'tiki' from the Marquesas Islands. From Karl von den Steinen, Die Marquesaner und ihre Kunst, Berlin, 1925.

323. Tattoo pattern from the Marquesas Islands. From

K. von den Steinen, same source as Fig. 322.

324, 325. Diagram of a t'ao-t'ieh and its composition. From W. Willetts, Foundations of Chinese Art, 1965, pp. 97 and 98.

326. Aztec earth goddess, 'Couatlicue'. About 15th century A.D., found 1790. Mexico, Museo Nacional.

327. Design on a Haida bracelet. From F. Boas, Primitive Art, Oslo, 1927, fig. 221. Reprinted by Dover Publications, New York, 1955.

328. Design on a Chilkar blanket. From Boas, same source as Fig. 327, fig. 204.

329. Lion mask from Lake Nemi. Ist century A.D. Rome, Museo Nazionale. Drawing by M. Wenzel after O. Kurz.

330. Ceramic dragon mask. 7th-8th century A.D. Cambridge, Fitzwilliam Museum. Drawing by M. Wenzel after O. Kurz.

331. Lion's head on a fortified gate. Detail from the manuscript of Rashid ad-Din's World History Istanbul, A.D. I3 17. Drawing by M. Wenzel after O. Kurz.

332. Detail of a Chinese ritual vessel. From Ancient Chinese Patterns, Hong Kong, p. 3.

333. Chinese coffin handle. 5th-4th century B.C. London, British Museum, by courtesy of the Trustees.

334. The Book of Kells, fol. 45r. Early 9th century. Dublin, Trinity College, reproduced by permission of the Board.

335. From the Psalterium Aureum. Late 1oth century. St. Gallen, Stiftsbibliothek, Cod. 22, fol. 91.

336. Initial from a lectionary from Reichenau. 10th century. Wolfenbüttel, Herzog August Bibliothek, n. 287o, fol. 41.

337. Initial from Jerome's Commentary on Genesis. School of Canterbury, early 12th century. Cambridge, Trinity College.

8Initial from Frenc Bible.Early1ctuy. Rouen, Bibliothèque Municipale, 8 fol. 305. Photo Courtauld Institute, © Professor C.R. Dodwell.

339. Opening from a Netherlandish Book of Hours. About 1300. London, British Library, Stowe 17 fol. 111v. Reproduced by permission of the British Library Board.

340. English sandstone corbel. 12th cntury. London, Victoria and Albert Museum.

341a. Villard de Honnecourt: Foliate heads. About 1235. Paris, Bibliothèque Nationale, Cod. fr. 19093, fol. 22r.

341b. Stall termination from Rattlesden, Suffolk. 14th century. From M. R. James, Suffolk and Norfolk, illustrated by G. E. Chambers, London, 1930, p. 83.

342. Misericord from Chichester Cathedral. About 1330. Photo Edwin Smith.

343. Perugino: Detail of the ceiling of the Cambio, Perugia. About I5oo. Photo Alinari, Florence.

344. Lucas van Leyden: Grotesques. Engraving, 1523. From P. Jessen, Meister des Ornamentstichs, I, fig. 166.

345. Master of the Die: Grotesque. Early 16th century. From Berliner, I, 47.

346. Cornelis Bos: Grotesque, 1546. From Jessen, I, fig. 175.

347. Hendrik Goltzius: Detail from an engraving of Bacchus with a cup. About 16oo. From Zülch, Entstehung des Ohrmuschelstils, fig. I.

348. Federico Zuccaro: Cartouche. About 1600. From Berliner, 11, 268.

349. Adam van Vianen: Title page of Constige Modellen van verschyden silbern vasen. From Jessen, II, p. 93.

35o. 'Allah' in a panel of enamelled earthenware tiles from the collegiate Mosque of el-Ayny, Cairo. 15th century. From A. Christie, Pattern Design fig. 71.

351. Benjamin Cunningham: Bent Space. 1965. Courtesy of East-Hampton Gallery, New York.

352. Jean-Baptiste Pillement: From the Cahier de Cartels Chinois. About 1770. From Jessen, 11, figs. 95, 105.

353, 354. Paul Klee: Drüber und empor. Drawing, 1931. Bern, Kunstmuseum.

senseoforderst00gomb

# THE SENSE OF ORDER A Study in the Psychology of Decorative Art E. H. GOMBRICH

"Gombrich's Sense of Order is a peer for his Art and Illusion, as wise and as witty, as revealing and wide-ranging. His explorations of the realm of design are splendid adventures, presented triumphantly. He is candid about his own ideas as well as those of others, and his magnificent illustrations call for our attention as much as his commentary calls for our serious thought."

Journal of Aesthetics and Art Criticism

"We are fortunate in having E. H. Gombrich to pursue the sometimes elusive relations between art and psychology. The Sense of Order once again proves that Mr. Gombrich's work is valuable. Not only is his knowledge of both art history and psychology almost encyclopedic, but he approaches his subject with a pragmatic point of view, explaining everything in the simplest terms, pointing out where we simply have no data, appropriating models from other fields when he finds them useful, and reviewing clearly what we do know.'

Antiques World

"Gombrich has attempted nothing less than a global analysis of the entire range of the decorative arts, from prehistoric epochs to the present. . . . [He] has made a brilliant and original contribution. . . . The Sense of Order is enhanced with color plates, black and white illustrations and photographs which explicate the text ànd clarify the argument. It will no doubt be as widely read and discussed as Art and Illusion."

Portfolio

"Gombrich stimulates and entertains. His own volume is an imposition of high order on the profusion of art books that offer a thousand views but not a single vision."

Time

"Mr. Gombrich is no run-of-the-mill art historian. Every feather sticks to his magnet. To read him is to be carried along by a euphoric mix of propositions ransacked from biology, psychology, semiotics, information theory, art. . . . Who else can impart such excitement to the zoomorphic shapes on a Chinese coffin handle or the symmetrical pattern of overlapping rosettes that turns up in Phoenician ivories and Assyrian pavements?"

Suzi Gablik, The New Tork Times Book Review

E. H. Gombrich was Director of the Warburg Institute and Professor of the History of the Classical Tradition in the University of London until his retirement in 1976.
